article.title: Patronage in California Law
article.para: The term "patronage" appears throughout the California Codes. Its legal meaning and implications diverge into two primary categories. The first category treats patronage as a neutral or positive concept representing a business's customer base, the economic activity of members in a cooperative, or the ridership of a public transit system. The second category treats patronage as a prohibited and illegal kickback for referrals, particularly in healthcare, workers' compensation, and disability insurance claims.
